subject: Build the Dataset First Protocol: a permanent knowledge-necessity governance family with an ontology, a corpus lifecycle and a detection engine wired into the prompt surface

prediction.claim: A corpus built from an ASSERTED thesis (no source dataset on disk), in parallel with an unfinished sibling corpus (SQI, 0 sealed Parts), will show a measurably LOWER citation rate from subsequent implementations than a corpus built from an established source.
prediction.observable: citations of vault/knowledge_base/dataset_first/* by implementation commits, over the next 10 builds routed through the DFP classifier

override.why: The CORPUS_OPEN signal returned DEFER (SQI open, 0 sealed Parts) and the source science did not exist on disk. Both were surfaced BEFORE any corpus was written; the Owner directed the build to proceed in a parallel pane with scope collapsed 7 datasets -> 3. NOTE: the sufficiency engine itself returned HYBRID, which is what was built -- the CLASS was not overridden, the DEFER signal was. The author's hand-scored expectation of DATASET_FIRST_MANDATORY was wrong and the engine was right (DFP-00 IV.15).

rationale: The founding self-exemption (DFP-00 Part IX). Recorded loudly rather than hidden: a protocol whose first act is to exempt itself silently is a fraud. The exemption is not the precedent -- the receipt is.
